A synthetic rubber is NOT obtained from the polymerization of?
solution: Synthetic rubbers are polymers of dienes and related unsaturated compounds. Neoprene (polychloroprene) comes from chloroprene polymerization; isoprene polymerizes to give polyisoprene (synthetic natural rubber); butadiene gives polybutadiene (Buna rubber). Hexane (Cв‚†Hв‚Ѓв‚„) is a saturated alkane with no double bonds and cannot undergo addition polymerization to form rubber.

question: The enzyme used in the hydrolysis of starch to dextrin and maltose is
solution: Amylase catalyses the hydrolysis of starch, breaking the glycosidic bonds to produce dextrin (intermediate) and then maltose. Salivary and pancreatic amylase perform this function in digestion. Lipase digests fats; invertase hydrolyses sucrose; zymase converts sugars to ethanol.

question: A quantity of electricity liberates 3.6 g of silver from its salt. What mass of aluminium will be liberated from its salt by the same quantity of electricity? [Al = 27, Ag = 108]
solution: Moles of Ag deposited = 3.6/108 = 0.0333 mol. AgвЃє + eвЃ» в†’ Ag (n=1), so charge = 0.0333 F. AlВівЃє + 3eвЃ» в†’ Al (n=3), so moles of Al = 0.0333/3 = 0.01111 mol. Mass of Al = 0.01111 Г— 27 = 0.30 g.

What is the product of the electrolysis of aqueous sodium chloride (NaCl) using inert electrodes?

solution: Time = 2 hrs 30 min = 9000 s. Charge = 2 Г— 9000 = 18000 C. MgВІвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Mg, so 2 Faradays deposit 24 g of Mg. Faradays used = 18000 Г· 96500 = 0.1865 F. Mass of Mg = (0.1865 Г· 2) Г— 24 = 2.24 g.

question: n monosaccharide в‡Њ (P/Q) polysaccharide + n water. In the process above, P and Q respectively represent
solution: In the forward direction (monosaccharides в†’ polysaccharide + water), water is eliminated вЂ” this is condensation polymerization. The empirical formula of an organic liquid hydrocarbon is XY. If the relative molar masses of X and Y are 72 and 6 respectively, its vapour density is likely to be
solution: Empirical formula mass = 72 + 6 = 78 g/mol. Vapour density = molar mass Г· 2 = 78 Г· 2 = 39.

What is the chemical structure of soap and detergent molecules?
solution: Soap and detergent molecules have a dual structure: a hydrophilic (water-loving) ionic head that dissolves in water, and a long hydrophobic (water-repelling) hydrocarbon tail that dissolves in grease and oil. This dual nature allows them to form micelles around grease particles, suspending them in water so they can be washed away.

question: In a flame test for calcium, the flame appears

option.a: Blue when viewed through a blue glass
option.b: Green when viewed through a blue glass
option.c: Orange-red when viewed through a blue glass
option.d: Brick-red when viewed through a blue glass

question: The colour of litmus in an alkaline medium is
solution: Litmus is a pH indicator that turns blue in alkaline (basic) solutions and red in acidic solutions. In a neutral solution, it remains purple. Therefore, in an alkaline medium, litmus turns blue.

question: Cathode rays cause an object placed behind a perforated anode to cast a shadow on the screen. This observation shows that the rays
solution: For a shadow to be cast, the rays must travel in straight lines from the source. If they were scattered or bent around the object, a sharp shadow would not be formed.

question: The most abundant rare gas in the atmosphere is?
solution: Argon (Ar) is the most abundant noble (rare) gas in the atmosphere, making up approximately 0.93% of dry air by volume. It is the third most abundant gas in the atmosphere after nitrogen and oxygen. Helium, neon, and xenon are present in much smaller quantities.

question: Addition of sodium chloride to water to form a solution would lead to?

option.a: increase in freezing point and increase the boiling point
option.b: increase in freezing point and decrease the boiling point
option.c: decrease in freezing point and decrease the boiling point
option.d: decrease in freezing point and increase the boiling point

solution: Dissolving a solute (NaCl) in a solvent (water) causes colligative property changes. The dissolved ions lower the vapour pressure of the solution, which results in: (1) depression of freezing point, the solution freezes at a temperature lower than 0В°C, and (2) elevation of boiling point, the solution boils at a temperature higher than 100В°C. These are called freezing point depression and boiling point elevation.

question: The heat of formation of ethene, Cв‚‚Hв‚„ is 50 kJ molвЃ»В№ and that of ethane, Cв‚‚Hв‚† is -82 kJ molвЃ»В№. Calculate the heat evolved in the process: Cв‚‚Hв‚„ + Hв‚‚ в†’ Cв‚‚Hв‚†

solution: Since methanoic acid (HCOOH) is miscible with water and has a similar boiling point, simple distillation will not separate them. question: An organic compound that does not undergo a reaction with both hydrogen cyanide and hydroxylamine can be?
solution: Hydrogen cyanide (HCN) and hydroxylamine (NHв‚‚OH) are nucleophilic reagents that undergo addition reactions with carbonyl compounds (aldehydes and ketones). Alkanal (aldehyde) and alkanone (ketone) both react with HCN and hydroxylamine. Alkanoic acids do not react with HCN or hydroxylamine under normal conditions. Alkenes lack the carbonyl group entirely and do not react with these reagents. However, the compound that reacts with neither is the alkene, as it undergoes electrophilic addition not nucleophilic addition to carbonyl groups.

Butane is Cв‚„Hв‚Ѓв‚Ђ; 2-methylpropane is Cв‚„Hв‚Ѓв‚Ђ; 2-ethylbutane would be Cв‚†Hв‚Ѓв‚„. Only 2-methylbutane matches Cв‚…Hв‚Ѓв‚‚.

Wrought iron is obtained by heating cast iron in a furnace with?
solution: Wrought iron is produced by heating cast iron (pig iron) with haematite (Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ) in a puddling furnace. The haematite oxidises the excess carbon and impurities in cast iron: Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ + 3C в†’ 2Fe + 3CO. This removes most of the carbon content, producing wrought iron which is purer, more malleable, and more ductile than cast iron.

In countries where the temperature fall below 273 K, salt is always spilled on the road in order to

solution: Salt (NaCl) is spread on icy roads to reduce the freezing point of water (freezing point depression). Dissolving salt in water lowers its freezing point below 0В°C (273 K), causing ice to melt even at sub-zero temperatures and preventing refreezing.

question: Which of the following is used to power steam engines?
solution: Coal is the fuel used to power steam engines. It is burned in the furnace of the steam engine to heat water and produce steam, which drives the pistons or turbines. Coal was the primary fuel of the Industrial Revolution and early locomotives. Lubricating oil reduces friction; bitumen is used for road surfacing; diesel powers internal combustion engines.

Which of the following substances is a mixture?
solution: A mixture contains two or more substances not chemically combined. Seawater is a mixture of water, dissolved salts (NaCl, MgClв‚‚, etc.), and other substances in variable proportions. Granulated sugar (sucrose), sodium chloride, and iron are all pure substances with fixed compositions.

The process that requires the use of hard water in its operation is
solution: Tanning of leather requires hard water. The calcium and magnesium ions in hard water react with tannins to help harden and preserve animal hides. Hard water is undesirable for laundry (wastes soap), bottling, and dyeing processes.

question: What is the main source of carbon monoxide (CO) in urban areas?
solution: In urban areas, the primary source of carbon monoxide (CO) is vehicle emissions. CO is produced by the incomplete combustion of petrol and diesel in internal combustion engines when there is insufficient oxygen. High concentrations of CO in cities are directly linked to traffic density. Volcanic eruptions and forest fires contribute CO but are not the main urban sources.

question: What is eutrophication?

option.a: The release of harmful chemicals into water bodies
option.b: The excessive growth of algae due to nutrient enrichment
option.c: The presence of heavy metals in water
option.d: The formation of oil slicks on water surfaces

solution: Eutrophication is the process by which a water body becomes enriched with nutrients, particularly nitrates and phosphates from agricultural runoff and sewage. This triggers excessive growth of algae and aquatic plants (algal bloom). When the algae die and decompose, bacteria consume large amounts of oxygen, leading to oxygen depletion and the death of fish and other aquatic organisms.

question: According to Charles's law, the volume of a gas becomes zero at
solution: Charles's Law states that volume is directly proportional to absolute temperature (V в€ќ T in Kelvin). Extrapolating the V-T graph to V = 0 gives T = 0 K, which corresponds to -273В°C (absolute zero). At this theoretical temperature, the volume of an ideal gas would be zero.

question: The elements that belong to the third period of periodic table are
solution: The third period (Period 3) contains elements with atomic numbers 11вЂ“18: Na, Mg, Al, Si, P, S, Cl, and Ar. Option D (Na, Mg, S, Ar) are all correctly in Period 3. The other options contain Period 2 elements (Li, Be, B, C, N, O).

question: The number of geometrical isomers of butene are
solution: Geometrical (cis-trans) isomerism occurs when a C=C double bond has two different groups on each carbon. For butene (Cв‚„Hв‚€): but-1-ene (CHв‚‚=CHCHв‚‚CHв‚ѓ) cannot show geometric isomerism because one carbon of the double bond has two identical H atoms. But-2-ene (CHв‚ѓCH=CHCHв‚ѓ) shows cis-trans isomerism вЂ” cis-but-2-ene and trans-but-2-ene. Therefore there are 2 geometrical isomers of butene.

question: An element having the atomic number 17 will resemble which of the following elements having atomic number as given below?
solution: Atomic number 17 is chlorine, in Group VIIA (halogens) with 7 valence electrons and configuration ending in 3pвЃµ. Elements in the same group have the same number of valence electrons and similar chemical properties. Atomic number 35 is bromine, also a halogen with 7 valence electrons (configuration ending in 4pвЃµ). Bromine therefore resembles chlorine most closely.

question:
On which of the following is the solubility of a gaseous substance dependent?
I. Nature of solvent
II. Nature of solute
III. Temperature
IV. Pressure
solution: The solubility of a gas depends on all four factors: (I) Nature of solvent вЂ” polar solvents dissolve polar gases better; (II) Nature of solute вЂ” different gases have different solubilities; (III) Temperature вЂ” gas solubility decreases as temperature increases; (IV) Pressure вЂ” by Henry's Law, gas solubility increases with pressure. All four factors are relevant.

question: An example of a polysaccharide is?
solution: Polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates made of many monosaccharide units joined by glycosidic bonds. Starch is a polysaccharide made of many glucose units. Dextrose, mannose, and glucose are all monosaccharides (single sugar units).

solution: The empirical formula is found by simplifying the ratio C:H:O = 6:12:6 = 1:2:1, giving CHв‚‚O. Empirical mass = 12 + 2(1) + 16 = 30 g/mol.

Which of the following types of bonding does not involve the formation of new substances?
solution: Metallic bonding occurs between metal atoms where electrons are delocalised in a "sea" of electrons. This type of bonding holds metal atoms together in a lattice but does not involve the formation of a new chemical substance. Covalent, co-ordinate, and electrovalent (ionic) bonding all involve the combination of different atoms or ions to form new compounds.

question: Ethene is prepared industrially by
solution: Ethene (CHв‚‚=CHв‚‚) is produced industrially by cracking вЂ” the thermal or catalytic decomposition of larger hydrocarbon molecules from petroleum into smaller, more useful ones. Steam cracking of naphtha or ethane is the main industrial route to ethene.

option.b: Makes the oil evaporate easily by dissolving it

option.d: Reacts with the oil to form an odourless compound

question: Using the metal activity series, the metal that can liberate hydrogen gas from steam is?
solution: Only metals above hydrogen in the reactivity series can displace hydrogen from water or steam. Iron is sufficiently reactive to liberate hydrogen from steam at high temperatures: 3Fe + 4Hв‚‚O в†’ Feв‚ѓOв‚„ + 4Hв‚‚. Copper, tin, and lead are all below or at the level of hydrogen in the reactivity series and cannot liberate hydrogen from steam.

Oxygen is absent in?
solution: Kerosene is a hydrocarbon mixture containing only carbon and hydrogen atoms, with no oxygen in its composition. Soil contains water and oxides, glass contains silicon dioxide (SiOв‚‚) and metal oxides, and cement contains calcium silicates and aluminates, all of which contain oxygen. Kerosene is the only substance listed that contains no oxygen.

Alkanes are used mainly?
solution: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons that burn readily in oxygen to release large amounts of heat. Their primary use is as fuels, methane (natural gas) for domestic heating and cooking, butane for camping gas, petrol for vehicles, and diesel for industrial engines. Hydrogenation of oils uses hydrogen gas with a nickel catalyst, not alkanes.

question: The acid used in making baking soda and soft drink is
solution: Citric acid is used as a flavoring and preservative in soft drinks, and reacts with sodium bicarbonate in baking powder to produce COв‚‚ for leavening. It is the common acid in both applications.

question: What is the common name for ethanoic acid?
solution: Ethanoic acid (CHв‚ѓCOOH) is the IUPAC name for the compound commonly known as acetic acid. It is the main component of vinegar and has a characteristic sharp smell. Formic acid is methanoic acid (HCOOH), propionic acid is propanoic acid (CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚COOH), and butyric acid is butanoic acid (CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚CHв‚‚COOH).

question: CuO(s) + Hв‚‚(g) в‡Њ Cu(s) + Hв‚‚O(g). In the equation above, the effect of increased pressure on the equilibrium position is that

solution: In this reaction, CuO and Cu are solids and do not contribute to the gas-phase equilibrium. The gaseous species are Hв‚‚(g) on the left and Hв‚‚O(g) on the right вЂ” 1 mole of gas on each side. Since the number of moles of gas is equal on both sides of the equation, a change in pressure has no effect on the equilibrium position.

question: Naв‚‚X в‡Њ 2NaвЃє + XВІвЃ». The bond between Na and X is likely to be
solution: The equation shows Naв‚‚X dissociating into 2NaвЃє cations and XВІвЃ» anion in solution. The formation of oppositely charged ions (NaвЃє and XВІвЃ») upon dissociation is characteristic of ionic compounds. Ionic bonding results from the electrostatic attraction between positively and negatively charged ions formed by electron transfer from the metal (Na) to the non-metal (X).

question: The enzyme that converts glucose to ethyl alcohol is?
solution: Zymase is the enzyme complex found in yeast that converts glucose directly into ethanol (ethyl alcohol) and carbon dioxide during fermentation: Cв‚†Hв‚Ѓв‚‚Oв‚† в†’ 2Cв‚‚Hв‚…OH + 2COв‚‚. Maltase converts maltose to glucose, invertase converts sucrose to glucose and fructose, and diastase converts starch to maltose.

question: Vulcanization involve the removal of
solution: Vulcanization involves the removal (or breaking) of carbon-carbon double bonds in rubber molecules. Sulfur reacts with these double bonds to create cross-links between polymer chains, converting soft rubber into harder, more durable material.

question: The pollutant that contributes to the depletion of the ozone layer is
solution: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) are the primary cause of ozone layer depletion. When CFCs reach the stratosphere, UV radiation breaks them down to release chlorine radicals, which catalytically destroy ozone molecules (Oв‚ѓ) in a chain reaction. SOв‚‚ causes acid rain; CO is a respiratory poison; NOв‚‚ contributes to smog.

question: In the laboratory preparation of ethyl ethanoate, the water present in the mixture is removed using
solution: In the preparation of ethyl ethanoate (ester), concentrated Hв‚‚SOв‚„ serves a dual role: it acts as both the catalyst for the esterification reaction and as a dehydrating agent to remove water produced, driving the equilibrium toward ester formation. Anhydrous CaClв‚‚ is not used as it can complex with the ester.

Water gas obtained from the gasification of coke is made up of
solution: Water gas is produced by passing steam over red-hot coke (carbon) at approximately 1000В°C: C + Hв‚‚O в†’ CO + Hв‚‚. The product is an approximately equal mixture of carbon(II) oxide (CO) and hydrogen (Hв‚‚). It is an important industrial fuel and is used in the synthesis of other chemicals. It is distinct from producer gas, which contains nitrogen.

question: The nitrogenous compound in dead materials in the soil is converted to
solution: During ammonification, decomposer bacteria break down nitrogenous compounds in dead organic matter (proteins, nucleic acids) and release ammonia (NHв‚ѓ). This is the first step in the recycling of nitrogen from dead materials back into the nitrogen cycle.

question: The composition of petroleum varies because it is a
solution: Petroleum is a mixture of many different hydrocarbons with no fixed composition. Because it is a mixture (not a pure substance), its composition varies from one source to another.

question: Strong acids can be distinguished from weak acids by any of the following methods, EXCEPT
solution: Litmus paper only indicates whether a solution is acidic or basic вЂ” it turns red for any acid regardless of strength. It cannot distinguish between a strong acid and a weak acid. Conductivity measurement distinguishes them because strong acids fully ionise and conduct electricity much better. pH measurement distinguishes them since strong acids have lower pH at the same concentration. Heat of neutralisation also differs between strong and weak acids.

question: Four elements P, Q, R and S have atomic numbers of 4, 10, 12 and 14 respectively. Which of these elements is a noble gas?
solution: Atomic number 10 is neon (Ne), which belongs to Group 18 (noble gases). Its configuration is 1sВІ 2sВІ 2pвЃ¶ вЂ” a completely filled outer shell. Atomic number 4 is beryllium, 12 is magnesium, and 14 is silicon вЂ” none of these are noble gases.

question: Which trace gas in the atmosphere plays a significant role in the greenhouse effect?
solution: Carbon dioxide (COв‚‚) is a trace gas (about 0.04% of the atmosphere) that plays a major role in the greenhouse effect. It absorbs infrared radiation emitted from the earth's surface and re-radiates it, trapping heat in the atmosphere. Nitrogen and oxygen make up most of the atmosphere but do not absorb infrared radiation significantly. Argon is chemically inert and does not contribute to the greenhouse effect.

question: The energy value of petrol can be determined by
solution: A bomb calorimeter is used to measure the calorific (energy) value of fuels like petrol. The fuel is burned in a sealed oxygen-filled vessel surrounded by water, and the temperature rise is used to calculate the heat released.

question: The constituent common to duralumin and alnico is
solution: Duralumin is an alloy of Al, Cu, Mg, and Mn. Alnico is an alloy of Al, Ni, and Co. The only element common to both alloys is Aluminium (Al).

solution: Sulphur is used in the production of matches as a key component of the match head, where it acts as a fuel that ignites easily when the match is struck.

Detergents are manufactured with straight hydrocarbon chains, is to make them
solution: Detergents with straight (unbranched) hydrocarbon chains are biodegradable because soil bacteria can break down straight chains enzymatically. Branched-chain detergents resist bacterial degradation and cause persistent foam pollution in water bodies.

question: What is the name of the process by which ammonia is produced on an industrial scale?
solution: The Haber process is the industrial method for producing ammonia by reacting nitrogen gas with hydrogen gas: Nв‚‚ + 3Hв‚‚ в‡Њ 2NHв‚ѓ. The conditions used are approximately 450В°C, 200 atmospheres pressure, and an iron catalyst. The ammonia produced is mainly used in the manufacture of fertilisers.

When water drops are added to calcium carbide in a container and the gas produced is passed through a jet and lighted, the resultant flame is called an?
solution: Calcium carbide (CaCв‚‚) reacts with water to produce acetylene gas (ethyne, Cв‚‚Hв‚‚): CaCв‚‚ + 2Hв‚‚O в†’ Ca(OH)в‚‚ + Cв‚‚Hв‚‚. When acetylene is burned in the presence of oxygen, it produces the oxyacetylene flame, which reaches temperatures above 3000В°C and is used in welding and cutting metals.

question: A solution contains 20g of solute in 180g of solvent. If the solvent is water, what is the concentration of the solution in terms of mass by mass percentage?
solution: Mass by mass percentage = (mass of solute Г· total mass of solution) Г— 100. Total mass of solution = 20 + 180 = 200 g. Percentage = (20 Г· 200) Г— 100 = 10%.

question: An example of an alkaline gas is
solution: NHв‚ѓ (ammonia) dissolves in water to form an alkaline solution (NHв‚„OH). HCl forms an acidic solution, NOв‚‚ forms acidic solutions, and Nв‚‚ is neutral. NHв‚ѓ is the only alkaline gas listed.

question: The above orbital diagram shown the electronic configuration of
solution: The orbital diagram shows 7 electrons with configuration 1sВІ 2sВІ 2pВі, which corresponds to nitrogen. The 2p orbital has three unpaired electrons (one in each 2p orbital), following Hund's rule of maximum spin multiplicity.

question: The mass number of a nucleus is?

option.a: Always less than its atomic number
option.b: The sum of the number of protons and neutrons present in the nucleus
option.c: Always more than the atomic weight

solution: The mass number (A) of an atom is defined as the total number of nucleons (protons and neutrons) in the nucleus. Mass number = number of protons + number of neutrons. It is always a whole number. The atomic number is only the number of protons, and the atomic weight is an average that may be a decimal due to isotopes.

question: The sulphide that is commonly used in coating electric fluorescent tubes is?
solution: Zinc sulphide (ZnS) is a phosphorescent material that fluoresces when struck by ultraviolet light or electrons. It is used as a coating on the inner surface of fluorescent tubes and cathode ray tubes because it emits visible light when excited by radiation. This property is called luminescence and makes ZnS ideal for such applications.

question: What is the IUPAC name for the following compound? HCв‰ЎCCHв‚ѓ
solution: HCв‰ЎCCHв‚ѓ is a three-carbon compound with a triple bond starting at carbon 1. The IUPAC name is prop-1-yne. The common name is methyl acetylene, since it is acetylene (HCв‰ЎCH) with a methyl group substituted on one end. Among the options given, methyl acetylene is the correct common name.

question: Crude petroleum is converted to useful products by the process of?
solution: Crude petroleum is a complex mixture of hydrocarbons with different boiling points. Fractional distillation separates these hydrocarbons by heating crude oil and condensing the vapours at different temperatures in a fractionating column, yielding fractions such as petrol, kerosene, diesel, and lubricating oil.

solution:

solution: Boiling points: CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚OH (ethanol) = 78В°C, CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚CHв‚‚Cl (1-chloropropane) = 47В°C, CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚CHв‚‚CHв‚‚CHв‚‚CHв‚ѓ (hexane) = 69В°C, CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚CHв‚‚OH (propan-1-ol) = 97В°C. question: Which of the following acts as both a reducing and an oxidizing agent
Alloys are best prepared by

option.c: reducing and mixture of their metallic
option.d: cooking a molten of the metals

solution: Alloys are best prepared by melting (fusing) the component metals together in the molten state and allowing the mixture to cool and solidify. This ensures homogeneous mixing of the metals at the atomic level, producing a uniform alloy.

question: The constituent common to duralumin and alnico is
solution: Duralumin is an alloy of Al, Cu, Mg, and Mn. Alnico is an alloy of Al, Ni, and Co. The only element common to both is Aluminium (Al).

In the laboratory preparation of oxygen, the gas cannot be collected by downward displacement of air because

solution: Oxygen cannot be collected by downward displacement of air because oxygen is itself a major component of air (~21%). Introducing Oв‚‚ into air simply mixes with the air already present, making it impossible to collect a pure sample. Oв‚‚ is collected over water (upward displacement of water) or by upward displacement of air instead.

What type of reaction is involved in the formation of alkanols from alkenes?
solution: Alkanols (alcohols) are formed from alkenes by the addition of water across the C=C double bond in a reaction called hydration. For example: CHв‚‚=CHв‚‚ + Hв‚‚O в†’ CHв‚ѓCHв‚‚OH. This is an addition reaction because atoms are added across the double bond without any atoms being removed. The reaction requires a catalyst (concentrated Hв‚‚SOв‚„ or Hв‚ѓPOв‚„) and heat.

option.c: Reacts vigorously on exposure to air

question: What accounts for the low melting and boiling points of covalent molecules?
solution: Covalent molecules are held together by weak van der Waals (intermolecular) forces between molecules. Little energy is needed to overcome these forces, resulting in low melting and boiling points. The strong covalent bonds within the molecule are not broken during melting or boiling.

question: Which of these reagents can confirm the presence of a triple bond
A particle that contains 9 protons, 10 neutrons and 10 electrons is
solution: The particle has 9 protons (atomic number = 9, so it is fluorine) but 10 electrons. Since electrons (10) > protons (9), the particle carries a net negative charge of -1. It is therefore a negative ion (anion) вЂ” specifically the fluoride ion, FвЃ».

question: A chemical widely used as a fertilizer is?
solution: Nitrochalk is a widely used nitrogen fertilizer consisting of a mixture of ammonium nitrate and calcium carbonate (limestone). It supplies nitrogen to plants in the form of ammonium and nitrate ions. Galena is lead sulphide (ore of lead), bauxite is aluminium ore, and emerald is a gemstone вЂ” none of these are fertilizers.

question: For a chemical reaction to be spontaneous, О”G must be
solution: The Gibbs free energy change (О”G) determines spontaneity. A reaction is spontaneous when О”G < 0 (negative). When О”G > 0, the reaction is non-spontaneous. When О”G = 0, the system is at equilibrium. The relationship is О”G = О”H в€’ TО”S, where both enthalpy and entropy contributions determine spontaneity.

The constituents of Alnico are Aluminium, Nickel and
solution: Alnico is a magnetic alloy whose name is derived from its constituent elements: Aluminium (Al), Nickel (Ni), and Cobalt (Co). It also contains iron as the base metal. Alnico alloys are used in making permanent magnets for electric motors, microphones, and loudspeakers due to their strong magnetic properties.

question: The process that occurs when two equivalent forms of a compound are in equilibrium is
solution: Resonance occurs when a molecule can be represented by two or more equivalent Lewis structures (resonance structures) that are in equilibrium or, more accurately, whose combination represents the true structure. The actual molecule is a hybrid of all contributing resonance structures. A classic example is benzene.

Ethanol, propyne, and benzene are not acidic enough to displace COв‚‚ from carbonate solutions.

question: When a few drops of Millon's reagent is added to egg-white solution in a test tube, the white precipitate changes to
solution: Millon's reagent (a solution of mercury in concentrated nitric acid) is a test for proteins containing the amino acid tyrosine. When added to egg white (which contains tyrosine-containing proteins), the white precipitate first forms then turns brick red on gentle heating. This brick red colour is characteristic of the Millon test for proteins.

question: A basic postulate of the kinetic theory of gases is that the molecules of a gas move in straight lines between collisions. This implies that

option.c: forces of repulsion and attraction are in equilibrium

solution: Gas molecules moving in straight lines between collisions implies there are no intermolecular forces acting on them during flight. The fact that they bounce off each other and the walls without loss of kinetic energy means the collisions are perfectly elastic. This is a key postulate of the kinetic theory of ideal gases.

question: The acid in electrolysis of water is dilute
solution: Dilute sulfuric acid (Hв‚‚SOв‚„) is used in the electrolysis of water because it's non-volatile, chemically stable, and provides ions for conductivity without interfering with the water decomposition reaction (2Hв‚‚O в†’ 2Hв‚‚ + Oв‚‚).

solution: Tollen's reagent (ammoniacal silver nitrate) reacts with terminal alkynes (RCв‰ЎCH) to give a silver acetylide precipitate. Alkynes have the general formula Cв‚™Hв‚‚в‚™в‚‹в‚‚. Note: Tollen's reagent also oxidizes aldehydes, but the question specifies hydrocarbons, pointing to alkynes.

Due to the high reactivity of sodium, it is usually stored under
solution: Sodium is stored under paraffin oil (kerosene/mineral oil) to prevent it from reacting with oxygen and moisture in the air. Paraffin is a non-polar, unreactive liquid that forms a protective layer around the sodium. Water would react explosively with sodium; mercury would form an amalgam; phenol would react with sodium.

question: Which of the following reactions would be expected to have the highest entropy change?
solution: Entropy (S) measures the degree of disorder in a system. The greatest increase in entropy occurs when a substance changes from a more ordered state to a less ordered one. The liquid to gas transition involves the greatest increase in disorder, as gas molecules have far more freedom of movement and randomness than liquid molecules. This change produces the highest positive entropy change (О”S).

question: Burning magnesium ribbon in air removes which of the following: (i) oxygen (ii) nitrogen (iii) argon and (iv) carbon (IV) oxide?

solution: Burning magnesium reacts vigorously with Oв‚‚: 2Mg + Oв‚‚ в†’ 2MgO, and also burns in Nв‚‚: 3Mg + Nв‚‚ в†’ Mgв‚ѓNв‚‚. Magnesium also reacts with COв‚‚: 2Mg + COв‚‚ в†’ 2MgO + C. Argon is a noble gas and does not react. Therefore Mg removes oxygen (i), nitrogen (ii), and COв‚‚ (iv) but NOT argon. The best answer from the options is D (i and ii only), as it captures the primary reactions.

solution: Cast iron, which contains about 3вЂ“4% carbon, is hard but brittle. It is used in making stoves, cookers, manhole covers, engine blocks, and pipes. Wrought iron (low carbon) is used for chains and ornamental work; mild steel is used for nails, rods, and sheets.

Which important nitrogen-containing compound is produced in Haber's process?
solution: The Haber process combines nitrogen gas and hydrogen gas under high temperature (450В°C), high pressure (200 atm), and an iron catalyst to produce ammonia: Nв‚‚ + 3Hв‚‚ в‡Њ 2NHв‚ѓ. Ammonia is the primary product and is used in fertiliser production.

question: A binary compound of oxygen with another element is referred to as?
solution: An oxide is a binary compound formed between oxygen and another element. Binary means it contains exactly two different elements, one of which is oxygen. Examples include water (Hв‚‚O), iron oxide (Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ), carbon dioxide (COв‚‚), and sodium oxide (Naв‚‚O). All these are classified as oxides.

question: The weakest attractive force that can be observed between two molecules is
solution: Van der Waals forces are the weakest attractive forces between molecules, arising from temporary dipoles and induced dipoles. These include London dispersion forces and dipole-dipole interactions.

All these are electromagnetic waves except
solution: A photon is a quantum (particle) of electromagnetic energy, not a type of electromagnetic wave. White light, X-rays, and infrared radiation are all types of electromagnetic waves within the electromagnetic spectrum.

solution: Option D is false. Catalysts DO alter the mechanism of the reaction вЂ” they provide an alternative pathway with lower activation energy. They also CAN appear in the rate law (rate equation), especially homogeneous catalysts whose concentration affects the rate. Options A, B, and C are all true statements about catalysts.

question: The reactions involving simultaneous oxidation and reduction are called?
solution: Redox reactions are chemical reactions in which oxidation and reduction occur simultaneously. One species loses electrons (is oxidised) while another gains those electrons (is reduced). The term redox is a combination of reduction and oxidation. These reactions include combustion, rusting, electrochemical cells, and many biological processes.

solution: Coal contains significant amounts of sulphur. When coal is burned to generate electricity, sulphur is oxidised to sulphur(IV) oxide (SOв‚‚). SOв‚‚ is the primary pollutant associated with coal-burning power stations. It causes acid rain and respiratory problems. Smog results from photochemical reactions, and CO is produced from incomplete combustion rather than coal-burning power stations specifically.

question: The number of electrons in the valence shell of an element of atomic number 14 is
solution: Element with atomic number 14 is Silicon (Si). Its electronic configuration is 1sВІ2sВІ2pвЃ¶3sВІ3pВІ. The valence shell is the third shell (n=3), which contains 3sВІ3pВІ = 2 + 2 = 4 electrons. Silicon therefore has 4 valence electrons.

question: A gas when mixed with oxygen, it produces a very hot and easily controllable flame. What is the name of the flame and where is it used?

option.d: Oxy-ethylene flame; cutting and welding metals

solution: Ethyne (acetylene, Cв‚‚Hв‚‚) mixed with oxygen produces the oxyacetylene (oxy-ethylene) flame, which burns at over 3000В°C. This extremely hot flame is easily controllable and is used industrially for cutting and welding metals. It is not used in miners' lamps or hunters' torches.

question: Sulphur exists in six forms in the solid state. This property is known as
solution: Allotropy is the existence of an element in two or more different physical forms in the same state. Sulphur exists in several allotropic forms including rhombic and monoclinic sulphur. Isomerism applies to compounds, isotopy refers to atoms of the same element with different mass numbers, and isomorphism refers to similar crystal structures in different substances.

question: The molar enthalpy change accompanying the removal of an electron from a gas phase atom or ion in its ground state is called?
solution: Ionization enthalpy (ionization energy) is the energy required to remove one mole of electrons from one mole of gaseous atoms or ions in their ground state. The first ionization enthalpy removes the outermost electron. Electron gain enthalpy is the opposite process, involving the addition of an electron to a gaseous atom.

question: The heaviest isotope of hydrogen is
solution: Hydrogen has three naturally occurring isotopes: Protium (В№H) with mass number 1 (no neutrons), Deuterium (ВІH) with mass number 2 (1 neutron), and Tritium (ВіH) with mass number 3 (2 neutrons). Tritium is the heaviest isotope of hydrogen. Positron is not an isotope of hydrogen вЂ” it is an antiparticle of the electron.

question: Treatment of cancer   II. Detection of leakages in water mains   III. Detection of the ages of ancient tools    Which combination of the above refers to the uses of radioactive isotopes?

question: The ions responsible for permanent hardness in water are sulphates of
solution: Permanent hardness in water is caused by dissolved calcium sulphate (CaSOв‚„) and magnesium sulphate (MgSOв‚„) вЂ” the sulphate salts of CaВІвЃє and MgВІвЃє. Unlike temporary hardness caused by bicarbonates, permanent hardness cannot be removed by boiling. It requires treatment with washing soda (Naв‚‚COв‚ѓ) or ion exchange to remove the CaВІвЃє and MgВІвЃє ions.

question: Which of the following is a common property of non-metals?

option.a: Exist as solids at room temperature
option.b: Tend to gain electrons in chemical reactions
option.d: Readily form cations in chemical reactions

solution: Non-metals tend to gain electrons in chemical reactions, forming negatively charged anions. This is because non-metals have high electronegativities and need relatively few electrons to complete their outer shells. High thermal and electrical conductivity are properties of metals. Most non-metals are gases or have low melting points at room temperature, though some like carbon (graphite) are solid. Forming cations is a property of metals.

The consecutive members of an alkane homologous series differ by
